text.title: rerank-before-reasoning Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for rerank-before-reasoning. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Retrieve 50-200 candidates with embeddings (recall pass) and rerank with a cross-encoder before passing top 5-10 to the reasoner; never feed raw embedding top-K directly to a strong-model reasoner.
rule.rationale: Pinecone and ZeroEntropy 2025 benchmarks report 10-20 pp precision@10 gains on enterprise retrieval suites; the same studies show that windows below 50 leave recall on the table while windows above 200 add latency without quality.

rule.statement: Sweep K ∈ {25, 50, 75, 100, 150} on a labelled eval set; pick the smallest K where precision@10 plateaus.
rule.rationale: Most production corpora plateau between K=50 and K=100. Going past the plateau spends reranker latency without recall gain; staying below it under-retrieves rare-but-relevant chunks.

rule.statement: Pick a reranker by running an eval suite drawn from your real traffic, not by reading a public leaderboard alone; public benchmarks systematically over-rank hosted rerankers on academic corpora that do not match enterprise traffic.
rule.rationale: ZeroEntropy 2025 reports rank flips between BGE-large and Cohere Rerank 3 across domains; per-domain eval is the only reliable signal.

rule.statement: Re-run the reranker eval whenever (a) the corpus has rotated more than 20% of chunks, (b) the embedding model is retrained, or (c) the reranker vendor ships a new version; do not re-evaluate on a fixed cadence.
rule.rationale: Calendar-based evals waste effort during stable periods and miss real shifts; corpus-rotation triggers track the actual signal.
